content.problemStatement:
You are given a **weighted** tree.

The distance from node xxx to node yyy is calculated as follows:


Let there be kkk edges in the shortest path from node xxx to yyy.
Let the weight of these edges, in the same **order** (from xxx to yyy), be denoted as e1,e2,…,eke_1, e_2, \ldots, e_ke1​,e2​,…,ek​.
Then, the distance from xxx to yyy is denoted as:

d(x,y)=f(e1,0)⊕f(e2,1)⊕f(e3,2)⊕…⊕f(ek,k−1)d(x, y) = f(e_1, 0) \oplus f(e_2, 1) \oplus f(e_3, 2) \oplus \ldots \oplus f(e_k, k-1)d(x,y)=f(e1​,0)⊕f(e2​,1)⊕f(e3​,2)⊕…⊕f(ek​,k−1), where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation, and

f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) denotes the integer obtained after bbb left circular shifts on the integer aaa (considering its 323232 bit binary representation).

For example, f((10000000000000000000000000000001)2,3)=(00000000000000000000000000001100)2f((10000000000000000000000000000001)_2,3) = (00000000000000000000000000001100)_2f((10000000000000000000000000000001)2​,3)=(00000000000000000000000000001100)2​.
Your task is to find ∑v=1v=Nd(i,v)\sum_{v = 1}^{v = N}d(i, v)∑v=1v=N​d(i,v) for each 1≤i≤N1\le i \le N1≤i≤N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the number of nodes.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​ and wiw_iwi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ of weight wiw_iwi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line, NNN space-separated integers, ∑v=1v=Nd(i,v)\sum_{v = 1}^{v = N}d(i, v)∑v=1v=N​d(i,v) for each 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the input forms a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.
